module.name: session
module.purpose: Owns the agent session runtime. session.rs defines Session { state: Arc<SessionState>, client_worker, host_worker }, whose new(name) stores the session name in a module-level SOURCE_NAME OnceLock, builds the Arc<SessionState>, and spawns the client and host workers via Arc::clone(&state).spawn_client_worker()/spawn_host_worker(); it also exposes the static package_message(SessionEvent) -> SessionMessage that stamps the stored source name. Below a // -- Private -- // marker, session.rs also holds the private behavior impl SessionState block whose methods all take self/Arc<Self> (never a separately passed state): parse_address, spawn_client_worker/spawn_host_worker (returning JoinHandles), run_client_worker/run_host_worker (per-connection current-thread tokio runtimes awaiting remoc recv and unwrapping message.event), create_task (packaging the CreateFailed event via Session::package_message and creating each Task via Task::new), and route_session_event/route_task_event. session_state.rs (renamed from session_context.rs) is the plain SessionState data struct: it holds the TaskId-keyed task WorkQueue plus the shared client/host remoc mpsc endpoints now typed as SessionMessage behind std::sync::Mutex, and defines only pub fn new() with no behavior methods. The local per-Task control channels stay SessionEvent. mod.rs is namespace and re-export wiring only.
